question: Given prepacked route entries (token, selected_slot) -> group, why not make group the outermost producer loop?
short_answer: Prepack makes group membership lookup cheap, but group-outermost contribution production destroys token-local Q/dO reuse. A compressed-group segment is a good writeback/reducer owner, not a good producer owner for the S4096 layer-2 route.

route_prepack_evidence.read: The prepacked route list is cheap enough to use. A naive group-panel kernel that rescans top-k is explicitly rejected; the route list should be consumed directly or fused into the producer.

group_outer_loop_reuse_evidence.interpretation: The group segments are excellent reducers: they collapse 12-24x writes and have useful segment lengths. But each group segment contains at most one selected row per token. Therefore a group-owned producer cannot reuse the same token's q/dO across the selected-K block; it sees many independent token rows instead of the query-major KxH by HxD tile.

math_contract.query_major_fast_path: For one token, load Q[token,H,D] and dO[token,H,D], then compute many selected-slot contributions with tl.dot/MFMA before writing dKV.
math_contract.group_outer_path: For one group, walk prepacked pairs [(token, selected_slot), ...] and sum grad_logits[token,H,slot] * Q[token,H,D] + probs[token,H,slot] * dO[token,H,D].
math_contract.why_group_outer_repeats_work: The same token contributes to hundreds of groups at layer 2. If groups own production, Q/dO for that token are reloaded or recomputed across many group segments instead of being reused once in a query-major selected-slot tile.

measured_negative_neighbors.note: Achieved the 23.81x write reduction but lost contribution-production efficiency.
Sorting by group plus row-wise recompute is decisively slower.
Owning contribution production by token/group panel collapses at S4096 even without dKV writes.
Closer to query-major but still pays per-contribution local-table overhead.

decision.reject: Do not make compressed group the outermost contribution-production loop for the hard S4096 layer-2 route.
decision.retain: Use prepacked group order for writeback/reduction ownership.
next_kernel_requirement: Keep Q/dO loaded in query-major tiles.
Compute selected-slot KxD contributions with tl.dot/MFMA-style math.
Use prepacked group metadata to do lane/warp/block segmented accumulation before global writes.
Emit compact (group, D16, producer_tile) rows or reduced atomics, followed by the already-cheap compact reducer.
